chunk_summary: This section outlines a standardized clinic workflow for consistent growth charting and documentation in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants post-discharge, emphasizing corrected age, measurement techniques, and z-score tracking to ensure accurate growth assessment and nutrition response evaluation.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of using corrected age for growth charting in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants up to 24–36 months.
Learn the standardized workflow for documenting growth metrics, including z-scores and centile changes.
Identify proper measurement techniques for weight, length, and head circumference in clinical settings.
Recognize the role of intake estimates (e.g., mL/kg/day, kcal density) in growth assessment.
key_facts: Use corrected age for growth charting through 24 months, extending to 36 months for extremely preterm/ELBW infants.
Consistently use one preterm growth reference until a defined transition point, such as term-equivalent age or a specific PMA.
Document z-scores for weight, length, and head circumference, noting any downward crossing (>1 z-score or two centile bands).
Measurements should include naked weight on the same scale, recumbent length on an infantometer, and head circumference using correct landmarks.
Pair growth data with intake estimates, such as mL/kg/day and kcal density for measured feeds or breastfeeding transfer observations.
reference_quote: Use corrected age for plotting and interpretation through 24 months (extend toward 36 months in extremely preterm/ELBW children when needed).
document_key_topics: Corrected age and growth chart transitions
Integration of intake estimates with growth data
